search:dotnet-core-performance-tuning:do.content:
- Use `dotnet publish -c Release` to compile your application in release mode.
- Implement caching strategies like Redis or MemoryCache for frequently accessed data.
- Utilize async programming with `async` and `await` keywords to improve responsiveness.
- Enable Just-In-Time (JIT) compilation optimizations by setting `<Optimize>true</Optimize>` in your project file.
- Monitor performance using tools like dotTrace, PerfView, or Visual Studio Profiler.
- Adjust the garbage collector settings by modifying the `gcServer`, `gcConcurrent`, and `gcAllowVeryLargeObjects` flags in your app.config or web.config.
- Optimize database queries to reduce latency and improve application performance.
- Use connection pooling for database connections to reuse existing connections.
- Implement lazy loading for collections and entities to reduce memory usage.
- Regularly update .NET Core to the latest version to benefit from performance improvements and security patches.

- Use `dotnet new webapi` to create an ASP.NET Core Web API project.
- Optimize database performance by using async EF Core methods like `ToListAsync()`.
- Configure logging with `appsettings.json` and set the log level for different environments.
- Implement caching strategies in your application to reduce database load.
- Use dependency injection (DI) for managing object lifetimes and promoting loose coupling.
- Monitor application performance using tools like Application Insights or built-in profiling features.
- Ensure thread safety by using locks, semaphores, or concurrent collections where necessary.
- Regularly update dependencies and .NET Core SDK to benefit from performance improvements and security patches.

search:dotnet-core-performance-tuning:op.content:
- Use `dotnet publish -c Release` for publishing optimized applications.
- Enable Just-In-Time (JIT) compilation by setting `<PublishSingleFile>true</PublishSingleFile>` in your `.csproj`.
- Utilize `GC.Server=true` and `ServerGarbageCollection=true` in the appsettings.json to enable server garbage collection.
- Implement caching strategies using `Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Memory` for data retrieval optimization.
- Monitor performance with tools like dotTrace, ANTS Performance Profiler, or VisualVM.
- Optimize database queries by indexing columns used frequently in WHERE clauses.
- Use async/await for I/O-bound operations to prevent blocking the main thread.
- Configure logging levels and sinks in appsettings.json to reduce log verbosity and improve performance.
- Regularly update .NET Core SDK and runtime to benefit from performance improvements and security patches.
